issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the default focus outline using `outline: none`. While it adds a `box-shadow`, this shadow uses a semi-transparent blue (`rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2)`) which may not be visible to users with low vision or in certain lighting conditions, and it lacks a high-contrast fallback within the same rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 645: `  outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 Level AAA requirement of 44x44px and the Level AA requirement of 24x24px is only narrowly met, but the small size increases the risk of activation errors for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 658: `  width: 44px;`, Line 659: `  height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links img
issuesFound.explanation: The social media icons have an explicit width and height of 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24x24px, making them difficult to activate for users on touch devices or those with tremors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1114: `  width: 24px;`, Line 1115: `  height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition to slide into view. While there is a global reset on line 435, the specific `transform: translateY(-200%)` on line 414 is not explicitly neutralized for users who prefer reduced motion, which can cause vestibular distress.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: `(fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script or addition of .skip-link { transform: none !important; } to the existing @media block)`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transition for `transform`. When a button is active (line 554), it translates 1px. This movement, while small, is not explicitly disabled in a specific override for this component.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: `(fix requires adding .btn { transform: none !important; } to the prefers-reduced-motion block)`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary CTA button has a transition for `transform` and moves on hover (line 748). This motion is not guarded by a component-specific reduced motion media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: `(fix requires adding .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transform: none !important; } to the prefers-reduced-motion block)`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards transition their `transform` property and move 2px on hover (line 841). Although there is a partial override on line 845, the transition duration itself should be zeroed out to prevent any perceived lag or motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 846: `    transition-duration: 0s !important;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" arrow icon transitions its `transform` property. This movement is not explicitly disabled for users with motion sensitivities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 886: `    transition-duration: 0s !important;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .step
issuesFound.explanation: The step cards transition their `box-shadow` and `border-color`, but the global reset on line 435 is the only protection. Component-specific transitions that might trigger layout shifts should be explicitly handled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: `(fix requires ensuring all .step transitions are zeroed in the media query)`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards transition their `transform` property and move 3px on hover (line 1048). The override on line 1052 only stops the hover state but does not address the transition property itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1053: `    transition-duration: 0s !important;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items transition their `transform` property and move on hover (line 1120). The override on line 1124 only stops the hover state but does not address the transition property itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1125: `    transition-duration: 0s !important;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards transition their `transform` property and move on hover (line 1220). The override on line 1224 only stops the hover state but does not address the transition property itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1225: `    transition-duration: 0s !important;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Links within FAQ paragraphs have their underlines removed (implied by the lack of `text-decoration` and the global reset). Only a color change and a font-weight increase are used to distinguish them from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with color vision deficiency.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1416: `  text-decoration: underline;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline to none. While it defines an outline-offset, it fails to provide a visible outline, border, or box-shadow within the same rule or a paired rule, leaving interactive elements without a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for non-keyboard focus without providing any visual alternative. This can prevent users with certain assistive technologies that rely on standard focus states from perceiving the active element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring-offset), 0 0 0 4px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default outline on focus. Although it adds a box-shadow on line 537, the shadow uses a low-alpha value (0.2) which may not provide sufficient visibility or contrast to serve as a reliable focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 534: #search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets scroll-behavior: smooth, but the global :focus-visible rule on line 411 sets outline: none. While a focus indicator is defined for :focus-visible, the base :focus state for the root element is suppressed without a fallback, which can interfere with focus management during anchor navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 411: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly sets outline: none on both :focus and :focus-visible. While it adds a box-shadow on :focus, it explicitly removes the outline on :focus-visible without providing the box-shadow or any other visual indicator in that specific rule, potentially leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator in some browsers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 622: `#search-input:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs (text, email, select, textarea) set outline: none on :focus. Although a box-shadow is provided on :focus, the :focus-visible rule on line 1059 repeats outline: 3px solid but sets outline-offset: 0, which may overlap with the border and reduce visibility compared to the global focus standard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1058: `.form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has an explicit min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 absolute minimum of 24px and the recommended 44px. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button has an explicit min-width and min-height of 20px. This falls below the 24px minimum requirement for touch targets, increasing the risk of accidental activations or missed inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 532: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px. While above the 24px minimum, it is significantly below the 44px recommended target size and lacks sufficient vertical padding or margin to compensate for the small interactive area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 601: `#search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons have an explicit min-height of 28px. This is a small target for primary navigation, making it difficult for users with limited dexterity to navigate the site accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 578: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This fails to visually hide the content, and because it lacks the standard absolute positioning and clipping properties, it does not function as a visually-hidden utility, potentially cluttering the UI or failing to provide the intended "hidden but announced" behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 441: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class defines transitions for transform and box-shadow. There is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable the transform (translateY) on the :active state, which can cause vestibular distress for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transition for transform: translateY(-2px) on hover. While there is a media query on line 754, it only targets the hover state and does not disable the transition property itself, which can still trigger movement during state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 754: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a complex 3D perspective transform and transition. The prefers-reduced-motion block on line 704 is empty and does not actually disable the motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets scroll-behavior: smooth, but the global :focus-visible rule on line 411 sets outline: none. While a focus indicator is defined for :focus-visible, the base :focus state for the root element is suppressed without a fallback, which can interfere with focus management during anchor navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 411: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly sets outline: none on both :focus and :focus-visible. While it adds a box-shadow on :focus, it explicitly removes the outline on :focus-visible without providing the box-shadow or any other visual indicator in that specific rule, potentially leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator in some browsers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 622: `#search-input:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs (text, email, select, textarea) set outline: none on :focus. Although a box-shadow is provided on :focus, the :focus-visible rule on line 1059 repeats outline: 3px solid but sets outline-offset: 0, which may overlap with the border and reduce visibility compared to the global focus standard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1058: `.form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has an explicit min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 absolute minimum of 24px and the recommended 44px. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button has an explicit min-width and min-height of 20px. This falls below the 24px minimum requirement for touch targets, increasing the risk of accidental activations or missed inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 532: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px. While above the 24px minimum, it is significantly below the 44px recommended target size and lacks sufficient vertical padding or margin to compensate for the small interactive area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 601: `#search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons have an explicit min-height of 28px. This is a small target for primary navigation, making it difficult for users with limited dexterity to navigate the site accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 578: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This fails to visually hide the content, and because it lacks the standard absolute positioning and clipping properties, it does not function as a visually-hidden utility, potentially cluttering the UI or failing to provide the intended "hidden but announced" behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 441: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class defines transitions for transform and box-shadow. There is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable the transform (translateY) on the :active state, which can cause vestibular distress for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transition for transform: translateY(-2px) on hover. While there is a media query on line 754, it only targets the hover state and does not disable the transition property itself, which can still trigger movement during state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 754: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a complex 3D perspective transform and transition. The prefers-reduced-motion block on line 704 is empty and does not actually disable the motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 704: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing pushes words closer together, significantly degrading readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative letter-spacing reduces the legibility of individual characters, which is a barrier for users with visual impairments.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em). Large headings with cramped letters are difficult to read, especially for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `animateHeight` function used by the FAQ accordion and other components toggles visibility but does not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger element. Screen reader users will not be notified that the content has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: `function animateHeight(el, expanding, trigger, duration = CONFIG.faq.animationDuration, onComplete = null) { if (trigger) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, expanding);`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the product grid layout between 'grid' and 'list' modes but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the buttons. Users of assistive technology will not know which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: `function _onViewToggle(e) { ... const mode = btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode'); qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => setAttr(b, ARIA.PRESSED, b === btn));`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product view mode is changed, the visual layout updates, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users who cannot see the screen will be unaware that the layout has changed from grid to list or vice versa.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2425: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.viewModeChanged(mode));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `newsletterForm` validation logic checks for a valid email but does not apply `aria-invalid="true"` to the input field when validation fails. Screen reader users will not be informed that the specific field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3315: `if (!result.valid) { setAttr(_emailEl, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl); ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module initialises the mobile menu but does not ensure the trigger button has `aria-expanded="false"` set on page load. Assistive technologies may not correctly report the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1985: `function init() { ... if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.clearField` function removes error classes but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute. A field previously marked as invalid will continue to be announced as invalid even after the user corrects the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3755: `function clearField(field) { ... field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID); removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 33: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation">`

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The `<section>` has `aria-labelledby="features-heading"`, but no element with `id="features-heading"` exists in the document. Screen reader users will not hear a proper label for this region.
issuesFound.fix:
Add the missing id to the heading within the section. Line 126: `

Everything you need to ship accessible software

`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button controls the visibility of the navigation menu but has no `aria-expanded` attribute. Screen reader users cannot know the current state (expanded/collapsed) of the controlled content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 34: `<button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Open navigation menu" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu">`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The "First name" input field collects personal data (`name="first_name"`) but lacks an `autocomplete` attribute. This hinders users, especially those with cognitive or motor disabilities, from auto-filling the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 545: `<input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on button
issuesFound.explanation: The button has no text content, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title. Screen reader users will hear an empty button, blocking access to its function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 47: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Open navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Link with non-descriptive text
issuesFound.explanation: The link's visible text is only "Read more", which does not convey its purpose out of context. Screen reader users navigating by link list may not understand where the link goes.

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> "Feature" is in a row with multiple column headers and lacks a scope attribute, making the table structure ambiguous for screen reader users.

issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm sets min-height to 20px, which is below the minimum touch target size of 24px. This can make it difficult for users with motor impairments or on touch devices to interact accurately (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size).
issuesFound.fix: Line 366: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Small interactive areas are hard to tap or click, especially for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 374: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: input[type="checkbox"] has width and height set to 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. This small size can cause selection errors for users with motor or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 830: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has transform and transition animations on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, providing no override. This can cause discomfort for users with motion sensitivities (WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions).
issuesFound.fix: In the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img, add: .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xl); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which are negative values. This reduces readability by making text too condensed, affecting users with visual or cognitive impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 248: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .hero-eyebrow
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-eyebrow has background-color: var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe) and color: var(--colour-info-dark) (#1e40af), with a contrast ratio of approximately 3.16:1, below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. This makes text difficult to read for users with low vision or colour blindness (WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast).
issuesFound.fix: Change colours to achieve higher contrast. Example: .hero-eyebrow { background-color: var(--colour-info-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-primary has background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) and color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff), with a contrast ratio of about 1.89:1, below 4.5:1 for normal text. This low contrast affects readability for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 341: .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. This relies solely on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text, which may not be perceivable by users with colour vision deficiencies (WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour).
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: a { text-decoration: underline; color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The function `toggle` in the mobileNav module changes the visible state of the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2154: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', _open ? 'true' : 'false');` after the `_open ? close() : open();` line.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onViewToggle` toggles the view mode between grid and list but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the button. Screen reader users will not know which view mode is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2247: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` for the active button and `setAttr(otherBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` for the inactive button(s).

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_updateCountBadge` updates the product count badge text but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users will not be notified of the updated count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2265: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.productsFiltered.replace('{n}', String(count)).replace('{s}', count !== 1 ? 's' : ''));` after updating the badge.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function `validateField` validates input but does not set `aria-invalid` on the field when invalid. Screen reader users will not be alerted to the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2748: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', !result.valid);` in the validation result handling.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have `aria-expanded` set on initial page load. Screen reader users will not know the initial state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: In the `init` function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the trigger element is selected.

Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent choice not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the user accepts or declines cookies via the cookie consent banner, the banner disappears without any announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that their choice was successfully registered, leaving them uncertain about the action's outcome.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13050: In the _onAccept function, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieAccepted || 'Cookies accepted');
Line 13070: In the _onDecline function, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieDeclined || 'Cookies declined');

fpTitles: Cookie consent choice not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element (_toggleEl) is given role="switch" but has no accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby). Screen reader users will hear only "switch" without knowing its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3665: Add aria-label attribute: if (!_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-label')) _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Switch between monthly and annual billing');

issuesFound.title: File upload zone has incorrect role="button"
issuesFound.explanation: The drag-and-drop zone element (zoneEl) is assigned role="button" but functions as a drop target region, not a button. This misrepresents its purpose to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3159: Change role to "region": setAttr(zoneEl, 'role', 'region');

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed via activeFilterChips.render(), no live region announcement occurs. Screen reader users are not informed when chips update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1810: Add live region announcement after render(): liveRegion.announce('Filter chips updated');

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing tablist structure and ARIA states
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons (data-filter-value) are not wrapped in a role="tablist" container and lack role="tab" and aria-selected states. This prevents proper screen reader navigation as a tabbed interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1651: Add role attributes: _tabsEl.setAttribute('role', 'tablist'); and in _syncTabAriaStates(): btn.setAttribute('role', 'tab'); btn.setAttribute('aria-selected', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing tab roles
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period buttons (_periodBtns) are not marked as tabs (role="tab") within their container, which acts as a tablist. This breaks expected screen reader navigation patterns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3672: Add role="tablist" to periodContainer and role="tab" to each period button in _syncToggleState(): btn.setAttribute('role', 'tab');

issuesFound.title: Toggle functions may not initialize aria-expanded on all triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-expanded="false" only if the attribute is absent, but some triggers may have invalid initial values (e.g., "true" when closed). This can mislead screen reader users about the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1348: Always set initial state: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Form validation error messages lack assertive announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When inlineValidator._renderResult() shows error messages, it updates aria-invalid but does not provide an assertive live region announcement. Screen reader users may miss critical error information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2887: Add assertive announcement: if (!result.valid) liveRegion.announceAssertive(result.firstError);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid on invalid fields

issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function of inlineValidator, when the result is invalid, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and when valid, set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: dropdown menu triggers aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.fix: Line 694: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a current page link but does not apply aria-current="page". Screen reader users navigating a list of links cannot identify the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1002: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component renders a current page link but does not apply aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify the current page within the mobile navigation list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1152: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose} aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility removes content from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, and `height: auto`, which does **not** hide content from assistive technologies while keeping it visible to sighted users. This defeats the purpose of a screen‑reader‑only utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override – .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes `transition: … transform var(--transition-fast);` which animates spatial movement, but there is no reduced‑motion override for this selector, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 300: `.btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }`  
  Line 820 (inside existing media block): `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override – .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of the primary CTA button applies `transform: translateY(-1px);` without a reduced‑motion safeguard, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 600: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); transform: translateY(-1px); text-decoration: none; }`  
  Line 830 (add to existing media block): `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em;`, which compresses word gaps and can reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without visual alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual cue (color change, border, etc.) is provided to differentiate links from surrounding text, making them indistinguishable for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` (or add a distinct color/border as needed).

issuesFound.title: Search input has no accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The site‑search `<input>` is given ARIA roles for a combobox but never receives an accessible name (no `<label>`, no `aria-label`, and no `aria-labelledby`). Screen‑reader users cannot discover the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1245: `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.searchLabel || 'Search');`

issuesFound.title: “Scroll to top” button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button identified by `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn` is made visible/hidden but never receives an `aria-label` or visible text. Keyboard‑only users and AT cannot know what the control does.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1082: `_btn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTopLabel || 'Scroll back to top');`

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab container missing required ARIA role
issuesFound.explanation: The element that houses the product‑filter tabs (`_tabsEl = qs('[data-filter-tabs]')`) is used with a roving‑tabindex pattern but is not given `role="tablist"`. Without the role, AT does not treat the children as tabs.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2103: `setAttr(_tabsEl, 'role', 'tablist');`

issuesFound.title: File‑upload drop zone lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The element passed to `fileUploadField.bind()` receives ARIA attributes for drag‑and‑drop but never gets an `aria-label` (or `aria-labelledby`). Screen‑reader users receive no indication of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4250: `setAttr(_zoneEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.uploadZoneLabel || 'Upload files – click or drag files here');`

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons missing `aria-controls` linking to their panels
issuesFound.explanation: Each filter tab (`[data-filter-value]`) toggles a panel of products but never receives an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the associated panel element. AT cannot associate the button with the content it shows/hides.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack initial `aria‑expanded` state
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons are toggles but the code only sets `aria-expanded="true"` when a panel is opened. When the page loads with the accordion closed, the trigger has **no** `aria-expanded` attribute, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their state on load.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the visible count badge is updated, but no ARIA live region is used, so screen‑reader users are unaware that the number of results changed.

issuesFound.title: Empty‑state visibility changes not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the filter, the empty‑state element is shown/hidden without any ARIA live announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the “no results” condition.

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The “previous” and “next” buttons for the testimonial carousel have no visible text and no `aria-label`, making them invisible to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button that opens the mobile navigation drawer has no visible label and no `aria-label`, leaving it unnamed for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an accessible name, for example:  
```html
<button class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Open navigation menu"></button>
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input lacks associated label or aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The email field in the newsletter form is referenced but never given a `<label>` element or an `aria-label`, so screen‑reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide a label or `aria-label`, e.g.:  
```html
<label for="newsletter-email">Email address</label>
<input id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" aria-label="Email address">
```

issuesFound.title: Main content region missing `role="main"`
issuesFound.explanation: The element identified by `#main-content` is used as the primary page region but no `role="main"` is set, which helps screen‑readers locate the main content quickly.

issuesFound.title: Document element missing `lang` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The page does not declare its language, preventing assistive technologies from selecting the correct language profile.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a `lang` attribute to the `<html>` element, either in the HTML template or at runtime:  

```js
document.documentElement.setAttribute('lang', 'en-GB');
```

issuesFound.title: Skip‑link target (`#main-content`) has no associated skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The page includes a skip‑link manager but no element with class `.skip-link` that points to `#main-content`. Users navigating via keyboard have no quick way to bypass repeated navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Add a skip link in the markup, e.g.: ```html Skip to main content ``` ---

issuesFound.title: Search clear button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button that clears the search field is referenced but not given an `aria-label` or visible text, leaving screen‑reader users without a description of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the clear button includes an accessible name, e.g.:  

```html
<button id="search-clear-btn" aria-label="Clear search">✕</button>
```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds the “open” CSS classes and locks scrolling, but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation drawer is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, _openInstance() adds the “open” class to the panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the associated button (`inst.trigger`). Assistive technologies therefore think the dropdown is still collapsed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open(item) method expands the answer panel and animates it, but it never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the question button (`item.trigger`). Users of assistive technology cannot detect that the FAQ item is expanded.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator bind does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (newsletter email)
inlineValidator bind does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “full_name”)
issuesFound.explanation: The “full_name” input is validated via inlineValidator.bind() but the validator never adds aria‑invalid="true" when the field is empty or too short. Assistive technologies cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the validation result handling for this field, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when invalid (same as Issue 4).  

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator bind does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “email”)
issuesFound.explanation: The “email” input is validated but the ARIA state is not updated on failure, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the error.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when the email validation fails (as in Issue 4).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator bind does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “subject”)
issuesFound.explanation: The “subject” input is validated but never receives aria‑invalid="true" when the rule fails. This prevents proper error announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') in the error branch for this field.  

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator bind does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “message”)
issuesFound.explanation: The “message” textarea is validated for length, yet the validator does not set aria‑invalid="true" when the content is too short or too long. This violates accessible form requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when the message fails validation (as in Issue 4).

fpTitles: inlineValidator bind does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “full_name”)
inlineValidator bind does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “email”)
inlineValidator bind does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “message”)

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in **inlineValidator** calls `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)` when a field fails validation. `markInvalid` only adds an `aria-describedby` reference; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, so screen‑reader users are not informed that the field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously‑invalid field becomes valid, `_renderResult` calls `srUtils.markValid`, which removes the error message but does **not** remove the `aria-invalid` attribute that may still be present on the field.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack aria‑controls when the drawer has no id (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.init()` only adds `aria-controls` if the drawer already has an `id`. If the drawer element lacks an `id`, the trigger button will have no relationship to the controlled region, breaking the required ARIA linkage.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer always has an `id` (e.g., `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-drawer');`) and then set `aria-controls` unconditionally.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks an accessible name (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger (`.nav-toggle`) receives ARIA state attributes but no visible text, `aria-label`, or `title`. Without an accessible name, screen‑reader users cannot discover the purpose of the button.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open routine does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: In **dropdownMenu._openInstance()** the panel is shown, but the associated trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"`. Users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: After opening the panel, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The button selected by `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn` is made visible/hidden and receives focus, but no text, `aria-label`, or `title` is provided. Without a name, screen‑reader users cannot discover the control.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle button (mobile menu) missing role="button" when it is not a `<button>` element (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: If the element matched by `SELECTORS.navToggle` is not a native `<button>` (e.g., a `<div>`), the script only adds `tabindex="0"` but does not assign `role="button"`. This violates the requirement that custom interactive elements expose a button role.

Mobile navigation toggle button lacks an accessible name (MEDIUM)
Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name (MEDIUM)
Navigation toggle button (mobile menu) missing role="button" when it is not a `<button>` element (MEDIUM)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing button role
issuesFound.explanation: The element that opens/closes the mobile navigation drawer is not a native <button> and the script never adds `role="button"` when it is a generic element. Without a button role screen‑reader users cannot discover that the element is interactive.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update `aria‑expanded` to true on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu is opened the script adds CSS classes and announces the change, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the toggle button. Assistive technologies therefore think the menu remains collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.open()` after the overlay handling add:  
    ```js
    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle never sets `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle is a two‑state control (open / closed) but the code never updates `aria-pressed`. Users of assistive tech cannot tell the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.toggle()` (or in `open()`/`close()`) add:  
    ```js
    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', _open ? 'true' : 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set `aria‑expanded` to true
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened the script adds the visible class but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` to `"true"`. This leaves the ARIA state out of sync with the UI.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `_openInstance(inst)` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden')` add:  
    ```js
    setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
    ```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack initial `aria‑expanded="false"`
issuesFound.explanation: For FAQ items that start collapsed the script never writes `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. The ARIA attribute is required so that screen readers know the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: Search input has no accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` receives ARIA autocomplete, controls, etc., but no label, `aria-label`, or associated `<label>`. Without an accessible name the field is invisible to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button that appears after scrolling has no visible text and no `aria-label`. Users of assistive technology cannot discover its purpose.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels missing `role="region"` when not a `<details>` element
issuesFound.explanation: The script only adds `role="region"` if the panel is not a `<details>` element, but it does not add it when the panel is a generic `<div>` without an explicit role. The panel therefore lacks a landmark role, preventing screen readers from announcing its content as a region.

fpTitles: Search input has no accessible name
Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name
FAQ accordion panels missing `role="region"` when not a `<details>` element

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions add/remove CSS classes and scroll‑lock the body, but they never set aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (SELECTORS.navToggle). Without this ARIA state, screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
In open() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); or toggle the attribute based on the _open state.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update aria‑expanded on their triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions animate the answer panel but never set aria‑expanded on the question button (data‑faq‑trigger). This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of which FAQ is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In open() add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close() add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button is not given an aria‑expanded attribute when the page loads (the menu is closed by default). WCAG 1.3.1 requires the state to be reflected in markup before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.init() or immediately after selecting _trigger, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are rendered with aria‑expanded only when opened via JavaScript; the buttons start without the attribute, so the initial collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqRegistry.parse() after creating trigger, set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); (and update to 'true' when item.open is true).

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() adds error messages but never sets aria‑invalid on the field when validation fails. Users of screen readers receive no programmatic indication of the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In inlineValidator.validate() after a failed validation, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and on success remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field is corrected, the validator removes the error message but leaves aria‑invalid set, causing a persistent false error indication.
issuesFound.fix:
In the success branch of inlineValidator.validate(), call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); or set it to 'false'.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown updates the UI but does not send an announcement to the shared liveRegion. Users are not informed that a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); or use a more specific message.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion expansions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened or closed, no ARIA live‑region update occurs, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open() and close(), add liveRegion.announce(`Question "${item.question}" ${item.open ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}`);.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module changes the visual state of the navigation drawer but never updates aria‑expanded on the .nav‑toggle button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: After the drawer is opened set navToggle.setAttribute('aria‑expanded','true') and after it is closed set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Trigger element missing aria‑controls reference to controlled region
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle button that opens the mobile navigation drawer does not have an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer element, violating the ARIA design pattern for disclosure widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Add navToggle.setAttribute('aria-controls', navDrawer.id || 'nav-drawer') during initialization (ensure the drawer has an id).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close functions do not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened or closed the code toggles CSS classes but never updates the aria‑expanded state on the button that controls the menu, so assistive technologies cannot convey the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance call setAttr(trigger,'aria-expanded','true') and in _closeInstance call setAttr(trigger,'aria-expanded','false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not set aria‑expanded on question button
issuesFound.explanation: The <button data‑faq‑trigger> elements are used to expand/collapse answers, but the script never updates their aria‑expanded attribute, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger,'aria-expanded','true') and in close(item) add setAttr(item.trigger,'aria-expanded','false').

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the code adds error‑message markup but never marks the field with aria‑invalid='true', so screen‑reader users are not informed that the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: After a validation failure call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true') (and optionally field.setAttribute('aria-describedby', errorEl.id)).

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: On successful validation the script removes error classes but leaves any existing aria‑invalid attribute on the field, causing persistent error indication for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: When a field validates successfully call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid') (or set it to 'false').

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle button starts with no aria‑expanded value, violating the requirement that disclosure widgets expose their initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init add navToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false') after the element is cached.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are given aria-haspopup but never receive an initial aria‑expanded='false' value, leaving the state undefined for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In register after setting aria-haspopup add trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false').

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in FAQ accordion are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened or closed the script updates the UI but does not write to any element with role="status"/ aria‑live, so users of assistive technology receive no notification of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> in the page (or reuse the existing live region) and, in open(item) and close(item), call liveRegion.announce(`Answer for ${item.question} ${item.open ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}`).

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function shows the mobile navigation drawer (adds CSS classes, removes body scroll lock) but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button (SELECTORS.navToggle). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button used to open/close the mobile menu is a <button> that toggles state, yet the code never updates aria‑pressed to reflect the on/off state. Assistive technologies lose the state cue.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle() (and in open/close) call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', _open ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load (mobile nav)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger for the mobile navigation (SELECTORS.navToggle) is never given an initial aria‑expanded="false" value in the markup or in an init routine. Users navigating with a screen reader encounter an element with no state information.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav initialisation code add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (dropdown)
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() opens a dropdown panel (removes hidden, adds CSS class) but does not set aria‑expanded on the associated trigger button. The ARIA state does not reflect the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and on close call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load (dropdown triggers)
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons (SELECTORS.navDropdownTrigger) are created without an initial aria‑expanded="false". The ARIA state is missing until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu initialisation loop, after creating each inst, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands an FAQ answer panel (adds CSS class, animates height) but never sets aria‑expanded on the question button. The accessible state is out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Inside open(item) add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in close(item) add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates not announced via live region (product filter)
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the product count badge, empty‑state message, and pagination, but no ARIA live region is used to announce these changes to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${visibleCount} products`); or create a hidden <div role="status"> to receive the announcement.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the invalid field (nor clears it on valid). ATs cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult() when result.valid is false add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and when valid, remove the attribute.

```

issuesFound.title: `Button` used in `NewsletterForm` does not communicate loading state (missing `aria-busy`)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit `Button` receives a prop `loading={isSubmitting}` but the `Button` component expects `isLoading`. Consequently the button never receives `aria-busy` or the spinner, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that a submission is in progress.
issuesFound.fix:
Use the correct prop name. Example fix:  
Line 860: ```tsx
<Button
  type="submit"
  variant="primary"
  size="md"
  isLoading={isSubmitting}
  aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
  aria-describedby={statusId}
>
  {isSubmitting ? 'Subscribing…' : 'Subscribe'}
</Button>
```

issuesFound.title: `Button` used in `ContactForm` does not communicate loading state (missing `aria-busy`)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit `Button` passes `loading={isSubmitting}` instead of the expected `isLoading`, so the button never gets `aria-busy` or the visual spinner, making the loading state invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Rename the prop to `isLoading`. Example fix:  
Line 1030: ```tsx
<Button
  type="submit"
  variant="primary"
  size="md"
  isLoading={isSubmitting}
  aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
>
  {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send message'}
</Button>
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop">` has `onClick` but lacks button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop `<div>` is clickable to close the mobile menu but is not a native interactive element and lacks `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and a keyboard `onKeyDown` handler, making it inaccessible to keyboard‑only users.
issuesFound.fix:
Convert the `div` to a button‑like element. Example fix:  
Line 540: ```tsx
<div
  className="mobile-nav-backdrop"
  role="button"
  tabIndex={0}
  aria-label="Close navigation menu"
  onClick={onClose}
  onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onClose(); }}
/>
```

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input missing error association
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> in the newsletter subscription form has an aria-describedby reference that points to an element ID that does not exist, so screen‑reader users cannot hear validation errors. This affects users who rely on ARIA error announcements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1190: ```tsx
          <TextInput
            id="newsletter-email"
            type="email"
            name="email"
            placeholder="you@company.com"
            autoComplete="email"
            value={email.state.value}
            onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
            onBlur={email.onBlur}
            required
            aria-required="true"
            invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
            aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error"
          />
```

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs missing aria‑invalid and aria‑describedby linking to error messages
issuesFound.explanation:
The `<TextInput>` components for name, email, subject, and the `<Textarea>` for message do not receive `aria‑invalid` when validation fails, and their `aria-describedby` attributes point to IDs that are never rendered, so error information is not conveyed to assistive technology.  
Fix (apply to each field, example for name field at line 1069):  
`<TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria‑required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria‑invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? "contact-name-error" : undefined} />`  
Also add corresponding error elements after each input, e.g.:  
`<ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} />` (similarly for email, subject, and message).

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto. issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block exists but contains no rules. Users who prefer reduced motion will not have animations/transitions disabled, potentially causing discomfort or disorientation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Use of deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets the `aria-dropeffect` attribute on the file upload zone. This attribute was deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1 and is no longer supported by assistive technologies. Its use adds noise to the accessibility tree without providing benefit.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5468: Remove `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'none');`

issuesFound.title: Contradictory ARIA live region attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The error message element is created with `role: 'alert'` which implicitly sets `aria-live="assertive"`. Explicitly adding `'aria-live': 'polite'` creates a contradiction in politeness levels that may confuse assistive technologies about how urgently to announce the message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4596: `role: 'alert'`, Line 4597: Remove `'aria-live': 'polite'`

issuesFound.title: Redundant ARIA state attributes on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons are assigned both `aria-pressed` and `aria-selected` states simultaneously. These attributes serve similar purposes (indicating selection/state) and using both on the same element is redundant and may cause inconsistent behavior in screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3258: Keep `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`, Line 3259: Remove `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on email input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription email input is identified in the code but no `autocomplete` attribute is applied. WCAG 1.3.5 requires autocomplete for personal data fields to help users complete forms more easily, especially those with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4813: `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl); if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attributes on contact form fields
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields (full_name, email) are bound for validation but lack `autocomplete` attributes. This omission prevents browsers from assisting users in filling out personal information, failing WCAG 1.3.5 for identifiable input fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4968: Inside the loop, add: `if (cfg.selector.includes('name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name'); if (cfg.selector.includes('email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attributes on wizard form fields
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard fields (wizard_name, wizard_email, wizard_card_name) are validated but do not receive `autocomplete` attributes. This prevents user agents from offering input assistance for personal and payment data, degrading the experience for users relying on browser autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5188: Inside the loop, add: `if (cfg.selector.includes('name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name'); if (cfg.selector.includes('email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); if (cfg.selector.includes('card')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'cc-name');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds classes and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds visual classes but does not update aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The paired _closeInstance sets aria-expanded="false", leaving assistive technology unaware the dropdown is open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and removes hidden but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in incorrect state reporting to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter updates aria-selected on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The highlightColumn function sets aria-selected="true" based on mouseenter events. aria-selected indicates selection state, not hover state, misleading screen reader users into thinking a column is selected when it is not.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField.bind creates redundant tab stop on zoneEl
issuesFound.explanation: The bind function sets role="button" and tabindex="0" on the drop zone container while leaving the native input[type="file"] visible and focusable inside. This creates two tab stops for the same action and may confuse assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal._reveal dynamic content not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _reveal function changes content visibility (likely from display:none or visibility:hidden) as the user scrolls but does not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. Users may miss content appearing dynamically.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator._animate dynamic content not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate function updates textContent with counting numbers dynamically. Screen readers may not announce the changing values or the final result unless an aria-live region is associated with the element.

fpTitles: fileUploadField.bind creates redundant tab stop on zoneEl
statCounterAnimator._animate dynamic content not announced

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded, as the ARIA state remains false or unset.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function within `dropdownMenu` removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not update `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This leaves the ARIA state out of sync with the visual state for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where opening is not announced via ARIA.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` utility function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to identify invalid inputs programmatically.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader _onScroll does not announce header state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onScroll` function hides or shows the header based on scroll direction but only emits custom events. It does not announce "Header hidden" or "Header shown" to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware of the layout change.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator _animate does not use aria-live for number updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function updates the text content of stat counters dynamically without an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users will not hear the numbers counting up or the final value change unless the element is marked as live.

issuesFound.title: searchResultsHighlighter highlight does not announce content changes
issuesFound.explanation: The `highlight` function modifies the DOM to wrap search terms in `<mark>` elements but does not announce that highlighting has occurred. Users expecting search feedback may not realize the results have been visually enhanced.

fpTitles: statCounterAnimator _animate does not use aria-live for number updates

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation `open()` function adds the open class and locks scroll but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets it to `"false"`, creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function hides the navigation drawer but does not call `liveRegion.announce()` to inform screen reader users that the menu has closed. The `open()` function includes this announcement, leading to inconsistent feedback.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button (hamburger icon). Keyboard users are left with focus in the body or lost, violating focus management best practices for modal dialogs.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init function does not initialize Focus Trap
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module declares a `_trap` variable but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap()` in the `init()` function. Consequently, the `open()` function cannot activate a focus trap, allowing keyboard users to Tab out of the modal drawer into the underlying page.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` function removes the `hidden` attribute and adds the open class but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The paired `_closeInstance()` function sets it to `"false"`, resulting in incorrect state reporting to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: Neither `_openInstance()` nor `_closeInstance()` in the `dropdownMenu` module use `liveRegion.announce()` to inform screen readers when a dropdown opens or closes. Dynamic changes to the page structure should be announced via a live region.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `faqAccordion` adds the `is-open` class and animates the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets it to `"false"`, causing a state mismatch for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner display
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner()` function displays the cookie consent dialog and traps focus but does not announce its appearance via `liveRegion.announce()`. Users relying on screen readers may not be aware the dialog has appeared until they encounter the focus trap.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._hideBanner function does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is dismissed (`_hideBanner`), focus is not returned to the element that opened it (e.g., `#cookie-settings-btn`). Keyboard users lose their place in the navigation flow after interacting with the banner.

fpTitles: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner display
cookieConsent._hideBanner function does not return focus to trigger
