issuesFound.title: Newsletter consent checkbox missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input for marketing consent has no associated accessible name. While it is wrapped in a label element, the label contains only text nodes and an anchor element, but the input itself lacks an id attribute to associate with the label's for attribute, and the label does not wrap the input directly (the input is self-closing and placed before the label text without wrapping). Screen readers will not announce the purpose of this checkbox.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1035: <input type="checkbox" id="newsletter-consent" name="consent" required aria-required="true">

issuesFound.title: Contact interest checkboxes missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox inputs for "I'm interested in" options have no accessible names. They are not associated with labels via for/id attributes (they lack id attributes), and while they are descendants of label elements, the label text is not properly associated because the input elements are not wrapped by the labels and lack the id attribute to match the label's implicit association.

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which keeps the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually. This utility class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers, but these properties fail to do so, causing visual clutter and potential overlap.

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query fails to disable animations
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not override transition and animation properties for users who prefer reduced motion. This fails to respect user preferences and may cause vestibular issues for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which causes spatial movement, but there is no override in the prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable this for users who prefer reduced motion. This can cause vestibular issues for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes a transform transition that is not disabled in a prefers-reduced-motion media query. Users who prefer reduced motion may still see the slide animation when the skip link receives focus, potentially causing vestibular discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — global :focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border in the same rule block. This removes the focus indicator for all keyboard-focusable elements that do not have more specific focus styles defined, making it impossible for keyboard users to see where focus is.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size for touch targets. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this small control.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size — footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which meets the minimum 24px requirement but falls below the recommended 44px touch target size. Increasing the target size would improve usability for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size — default button
issuesFound.explanation: The default .btn class has a min-height of 32px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended touch target size. While padding may increase the clickable area, explicit sizing below 44px can impede users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size — small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class has a min-height of 36px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended touch target size. Small button variants should still meet the larger target size for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide the content. Content marked with this class remains visible on screen while being announced to screen readers, creating a confusing experience for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override — button transitions
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class defines transitions including transform properties, and the :active state uses transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable these spatial animations for users who prefer reduced motion, which can cause vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override — skip link transition
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes a transition on the transform property. Without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override, users who have requested reduced motion will still see the animation, which may cause discomfort for those with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — secondary text on white background
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background. This produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision will have difficulty reading this content.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — section header paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text contrast (4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — products section header text

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes the default browser focus ring for all keyboard-focused elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to track focus position unless each element defines a custom focus style.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline from :focus-visible without providing a replacement. In high contrast mode, focus indicators are critical for visibility, and removing them entirely creates a barrier for users relying on forced colors.

issuesFound.title: Button component focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons have focus.

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu interactive elements focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible and #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible selectors remove outline without replacement. Navigation links and buttons show no visible focus state.

issuesFound.title: Submenu links focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset and border-radius, which do not create a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes outline without providing a visible alternative focus style.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement, leaving no visible focus indicator for these links.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector in the mobile media query removes outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation menu links focus indicator removed

issuesFound.title: Mobile submenu links focus indicator removed

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are particularly difficult to hit when small.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.

This meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended size for AAA compliance, and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and other properties that do not visually hide content. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, breaking the visual layout and potentially confusing sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Button component lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property but has no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animations.

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition without a reduced-motion override to disable the animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transition on transform properties, and the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 766 is empty, failing to disable the animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link animation lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after selector has a transition on transform without a reduced-motion override, causing the arrow animation to persist for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Step component lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .step selector has transition properties without a reduced-motion override to disable animations for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs lack reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector has transition properties without a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually. This fails to hide content from sighted users while keeping it accessible to screen readers, and may cause layout issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 369-379: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Button transitions not disabled for reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transitions for transform and other properties that create motion, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable these animations. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 524-530: `.btn { ... transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); ... }` and add at line 353: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Hero image transitions not disabled for reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector defines a transition for transform and box-shadow, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 745-748 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users sensitive to animation may experience discomfort from the perspective transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 745-748: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to appear merged together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 331: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Blockquote text contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.35:1. This fails the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making the quoted text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 330: `blockquote { border-left: 4px solid var(--colour-border-brand); margin: 0; padding-left: var(--space-6); font-style: italic; color: var(--colour-neutral-700); }`

issuesFound.title: Skip link text contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector uses color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (white #ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.73:1. This fails the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text (16px), making the skip link difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417: `.skip-link { ... background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: #000000; ... }` or use a darker background color that provides 4.5:1 contrast with white.

issuesFound.title: Hero primary button text contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector uses color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (white) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.73:1. This fails the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text (13.5px), making the button label difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 652: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: #000000; ... }` or darken the background color to meet 4.5:1 contrast.

Users with color vision deficiencies may not recognize these as links.

issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets text-decoration: none, and the hover state at line 1261 only changes color without providing a non-color visual differentiator such as underline or border-bottom. Users who cannot perceive color changes may not recognize these as interactive links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1261: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the page, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Within the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. High contrast mode users rely on system focus indicators, and this rule removes them entirely without replacement.

issuesFound.title: Button component focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on these navigation links.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. The card links rely solely on browser default focus styles which are removed here.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. The FAQ accordion buttons lack visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Footer brand link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Footer brand links lack visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Social links focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. The scrollable table region lacks a visible focus indicator when focused via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size

issuesFound.title: Screen reader utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, visible overflow, and auto dimensions instead of absolute positioning and clipping. This makes the content visible to sighted users while also potentially removing it from the accessibility tree depending on browser implementation.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on hover. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 580 is empty and does not disable this animation, which can cause vestibular issues for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Feature card transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card selector includes transform in its transition, creating spatial movement on hover. The reduced motion override only sets transform: none on hover but does not remove the transition property, which may still cause animation.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link arrow transform missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after selector transitions transform for the arrow indicator. The reduced motion override only sets transform: none but does not disable the transition itself.

issuesFound.title: Stat item transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item selector includes transform in its transition. The reduced motion override only sets transform: none on hover but does not remove the transition property.

issuesFound.title: Product card transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card selector includes transform in its transition. The reduced motion override only sets transform: none on hover but does not remove the transition property.

issuesFound.title: Pricing card transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card selector includes transform in its transition. The reduced motion override only sets transform: none on hover but does not remove the transition property.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and violates text spacing requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 175:
  ```css
    word-spacing: normal;
  ```

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight change. Links are only distinguished by color, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner focus trap prevents Escape key dismissal
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner creates a focus trap with escapeDeactivates: false, preventing users from closing the banner using the Escape key. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as users must be able to dismiss modal content using standard keyboard conventions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2847: Change { escapeDeactivates: false } to { escapeDeactivates: true } in the createFocusTrap options object.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner does not return focus to trigger element on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is opened via the "Manage cookie settings" button, closing the banner (via Accept/Decline) does not return focus to the triggering button. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and disorients keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2830: Store the triggering element reference (e.g., this._triggerEl = btn) before calling _showBanner(). Line 2860: In _hideBanner(), add logic to return focus: if (this._triggerEl && this._triggerEl.focus) { this._triggerEl.focus(); }.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout (dur) without providing a mechanism to pause, stop, or hide them. This violates WCAG 2.2.2 (Timing Adjustable) as users may need more time to read the notification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2154: Add hover/touch event listeners to the toast element that clear the timeout on mouseenter/focusin and restart it on mouseleave/focusout. Example: Line 2154-2156: Replace setTimeout with a pausable timer implementation that listens for pointer events.

issuesFound.title: File upload validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails (size/type), the code announces the error via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input element. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3117: After the liveRegion.announceAssertive call, add binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and ensure it is cleared to 'false' in the _onChange handler when valid files are selected.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button accessible name not ensured
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization queries the toggle button but does not verify or ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label or text content). If the HTML omits this, the button will be announced without a name, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1474: After querying _trigger, add: if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_trigger.textContent.trim()) { _trigger.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Open navigation menu'); }.

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard incorrectly uses role="tabpanel" for sequential steps
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard assigns role="tabpanel" to step panels, implying a tab interface pattern. However, the wizard uses sequential navigation (Next/Previous), not tab selection. This incorrect role confuses assistive technologies about the interaction model, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Role).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3324: Remove step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel'); and instead manage visibility using aria-hidden or ensure the steps are properly labeled regions without the tabpanel role.

issuesFound.title: Character counter live region excessively verbose
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter updates its aria-live region on every input event, announcing the remaining character count. This creates excessive verbosity for screen reader users, violating WCAG 5.2.2 (Timing) best practices for polite announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2654: Debounce the _update function (e.g., using debounce with 500ms delay) so that announcements are only made after the user pauses typing, rather than on every keystroke.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateProgress function sets aria-valuenow but does not ensure aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax are set on the progress bar element. While defaults exist, explicit values are required for robust accessibility, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3270: Add _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0'); and _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100'); (or dynamically based on total steps) to ensure the progress range is explicitly defined.

issuesFound.title: Toast notification auto-dismiss timer lacks pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager module sets a setTimeout to auto-dismiss toasts after a duration (dur), but provides no mechanism for users to pause or stop this timer on hover or focus. Users with cognitive disabilities or using screen readers may not have sufficient time to read the notification before it disappears.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch applies aria-checked to button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function sets aria-checked on the _toggleEl element regardless of its role. When _toggleEl is a native <button> element (which the code explicitly checks for but does not prevent aria-checked on), this is invalid ARIA. Buttons must use aria-pressed to indicate pressed state, not aria-checked.

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard step panels lack required tablist structure
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard assigns role="tabpanel" to step panels, but there are no corresponding elements with role="tablist" or role="tab". The tabpanel role requires an owning tablist and associated tabs to form a complete tab interface pattern.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel dots container lacks role="tablist"
issuesFound.explanation: The _buildDots function creates buttons with role="tab" but does not ensure the parent container (_dotsEl) has role="tablist". Screen readers require the tablist role to properly announce the tab navigation structure.

issuesFound.title: Site search clear button lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (_clearBtn) is selected from the DOM but never provided with an accessible name via aria-label. If the HTML does not contain text content or an aria-label, the button will be announced as "button" without context.

issuesFound.title: Site search input lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element is configured with role="combobox" and various ARIA attributes, but the JavaScript does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby). Screen reader users may not know the purpose of the input field.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function ensures aria-expanded and aria-controls are set on the trigger button, but does not verify or ensure the button has an accessible name (text content or aria-label). A button without a name is announced only as "button" by screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function ensures aria-haspopup, aria-expanded, and aria-controls are set, but does not verify the trigger button has an accessible name. If the HTML lacks text content or aria-label, the button will be unnamed.

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode buttons missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) only receive aria-pressed attributes when clicked via _onViewToggle. On initial page load, these buttons lack aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot convey which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2341: Add initialization loop for view mode buttons:
  Line 2341: qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)); });

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison expand button missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table expand/collapse button (data-expand-comparison) is not initialized with aria-expanded when the module initializes. Since the table is likely collapsed by default, the button should have aria-expanded="false" to indicate the current state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2729: After setting up event listeners, initialize the expand button state:
  Line 2729: const expandBtn = qs('[data-expand-comparison]'); if (expandBtn) setExpanded(expandBtn, false, _tableEl);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (_drawer) acts as a modal dialog that traps focus and blocks interaction with the rest of the page, but it lacks aria-modal="true". Screen readers may not announce it as a modal, and users might not understand that the underlying content is inert.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1427: After obtaining _drawer reference, add:
  Line 1427: if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer element (nav menu) lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Since it acts as a landmark region and modal dialog, it needs an accessible name to distinguish it from other navigation regions (like the account navigation).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1427: After obtaining _drawer reference, add:
  Line 1427: if (_drawer && !_drawer.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_drawer.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-label', 'Main navigation');

issuesFound.title: Search form missing role and accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search form element (_formEl) is queried but not ensured to have role="search" or an aria-label. Without these, screen reader users may not recognize the region as a search landmark, reducing navigation efficiency.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2060: After obtaining _formEl, add:
  Line 2060: if (_formEl) { if (!_formEl.getAttribute('role')) setAttr(_formEl, 'role', 'search'); if (!_formEl.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_formEl.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) setAttr(_formEl, 'aria-label', 'Site search'); }

issuesFound.title: FAQ list container missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ list container (_containerEl) receives an id for aria-controls linkage but does not receive an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Since it is a distinct region controlling the FAQ items, a label helps screen readers identify the purpose of the region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2888: After ensuring the container id, add:
  Line 2888: if (!_containerEl.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_containerEl.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) setAttr(_containerEl, 'aria-label', 'Frequently asked questions');

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs container missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs container (_tabsEl) is initialized with roving tabindex but lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. As a tablist widget controlling the product grid view, it requires an accessible name to identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2316: After obtaining _tabsEl, add:
  Line 2316: if (_tabsEl && !_tabsEl.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_tabsEl.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) setAttr(_tabsEl, 'aria-label', 'Product filters');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to show the mobile navigation drawer but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will continue to announce the button as "collapsed" even while the menu is open, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1772: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the function removes the 'hidden' attribute and adds an open class to the panel, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the associated menu is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1927: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function marks the FAQ item as open and shows the panel via animateHeight, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent accessibility state where open items are not announced as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3427: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, this function adds the error message to aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, violating WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification and 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1296: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus management when opening
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function displays the off-canvas navigation drawer but neither moves focus into the drawer nor activates the focus trap (the _trap variable is never initialized or activated). Keyboard users remain on the trigger button while the drawer opens, and focus is not contained within the drawer, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-haspopup
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, the code ensures aria-expanded and aria-controls on the navigation toggle, but does not add aria-haspopup="true" (or "menu"). This attribute is required to indicate that the button opens a menu, helping assistive technologies understand the control's behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1809: Add `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-haspopup')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');` alongside the existing aria-expanded check.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons misuse aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function sets aria-selected on the billing period buttons. The aria-selected attribute is not appropriate for native button elements unless they have a role that supports it (such as role="tab" or role="option"). These buttons should use aria-pressed to indicate the selected state, or be given role="tab" if they are part of a tab interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2679: Change `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` to `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` or add `setAttr(btn, 'role', 'tab');` before setting aria-selected.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2328: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the call to `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but fails to set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern and creates an asymmetry with _closeInstance which correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2482: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid helper function associates an error message with a field using aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true". Screen readers rely on the aria-invalid attribute to convey the validation error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1418: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function, immediately after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable intended for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). Consequently, when the navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab out of the modal context and lose their place.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2380 (inside init function): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` and Line 2329 (inside open function): Add `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels without announcing state changes to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology receive no auditory feedback when submenus expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2485 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` and Line 2500 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the close() function does not announce the closed state to screen readers. Users may remain unaware that the menu has closed, particularly if focus management is not immediately apparent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2360 (in close function): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function in the FAQ accordion collapses answer panels but fails to announce the collapsed state to screen readers. Users are not notified that content has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3570 (in close function): Insert `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` immediately before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step panels incorrect ARIA role
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard initializes step panels with role="tabpanel", but there is no corresponding tablist structure or tab elements. This creates an invalid ARIA relationship and confuses assistive technologies expecting a tabbed interface rather than a linear wizard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4030: Change `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` to `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'region');` or remove the role attribute entirely.

fpTitles: multiStepWizard step panels incorrect ARIA role

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the mobile navigation drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true", so screen readers cannot perceive that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2782: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, leaving keyboard users without context of the opened content and requiring them to manually navigate into the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2795: After `_lockScroll();` add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, disorienting keyboard users who must navigate back to the trigger manually.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap instantiation and activation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable but never instantiates `createFocusTrap` or activates it, allowing keyboard focus to escape to background elements when the drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2785: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` immediately after opening the drawer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens a dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing screen reader users from knowing the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2955: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands a FAQ item but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true", so assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4205: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open missing aria-expanded update on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion panel opens, the function does not set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element, leaving screen reader users unaware that suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3405: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself, so screen readers cannot identify the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1252: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` at the beginning of the markInvalid function body.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and dropdownMenu._closeInstance() functions show and hide dropdown panels by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger elements. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which dropdown menus are currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix:

  Line 1340 (in _closeInstance function, after inst.open = false): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up the mobile navigation component but does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute to "false" on the trigger button when the page loads. Screen readers may not correctly identify the initial collapsed state of the menu before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210 (in init function, after _initialised = true): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function configures the search input as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the input element when the suggestion list is closed on page load. Screen readers may not correctly convey the collapsed state of the autocomplete suggestions before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3280 (after const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function associates error messages with form fields using aria-describedby, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid to users when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 850 (in markInvalid function, before addDescribedBy call): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid clearance on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes error descriptions from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to false. The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers even after the user corrects the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 860 (in markValid function, after the if block handling aria-describedby): Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function dynamically updates the visible product cards and count badge when filters are applied, but does not announce the results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed when the filtered content changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3950 (in the filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips.render() function dynamically adds and removes filter chip elements from the DOM when filters are changed, but does not announce these changes to screen readers. Users may not be aware that a filter has been added or removed from the active filter set.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4150 (in render function, after _containerEl.removeAttribute('hidden')): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Active filters: ${chips.map(c => c.label).join(', ')}`);` or announce the count of remaining filters.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the hamburger trigger button. Since the drawer is closed by default on page load, the trigger must communicate this initial state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2218: Insert `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after the line `_initialised = true;` inside the `init` function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable for focus management but never instantiates it. When the drawer opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay. Additionally, focus is not returned to the trigger button on close.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2132: Change `let _trap = null;` to initialize the trap after DOM elements are found in `init`: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`

  Line 2178: Insert `_trap.deactivate();` immediately after `_unlockScroll();` inside the `close` function (before emitting the close event).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded

This prevents screen reader users from knowing when a submenu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded

This leaves screen reader users unaware of the current expanded/collapsed state after interaction.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validation

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content updates not announced

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing initial aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.fix: Line (after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, aria-invalid is not cleared (set to "false" or removed), potentially leaving screen readers with stale invalid state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close do not announce state changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer does not trigger a live region announcement, so screen reader users are not informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open() before closing brace): `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`
  Line (in close() before closing brace): `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the results count is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware that the product grid has updated and how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods does not trigger a live region announcement, so screen reader users are not informed of the price change context.

productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instance toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by setting the hidden attribute and toggling CSS classes, but it never updates aria-expanded on the trigger button to reflect the state change. Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded or collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1253: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 1266: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside `_closeInstance` before or after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: While the faqAccordion close() function sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, the open() function does not set aria-expanded="true". This asymmetry means screen readers will not announce when an FAQ item is expanded, leaving users unaware that content has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2112: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers may not convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 815: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 828: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` at the end of the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the results count badge is updated visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region. Users are not informed that the product list has been filtered or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1985: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse without announcing the state change to screen reader users. Users may not realize that new content has appeared or that content has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2112: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` at the end of the open function. Line 2132: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` at the end of the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Screen readers may not recognize the button as controlling an expandable menu until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1125: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are registered and initialised with aria-haspopup, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially. The attribute only appears after the first toggle, causing inconsistent state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1230: After `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-haspopup')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');`, add `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is configured with role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox", but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" on page load. Screen readers may not correctly identify the collapsible nature of the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: After mounting the listbox and setting up the input, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component ignores children prop in NewsletterForm
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component is called with a child text node ("Something went wrong..."), but the component implementation only renders the `message` prop and ignores `children`. Screen-reader users will not hear the server-error announcement because the element renders empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1053: `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." role="alert" />`

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component ignores children prop in ContactForm
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component is called with a child text node ("Submission failed..."), but the component implementation only renders the `message` prop and ignores `children`. The error will not be announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1153: `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." role="alert" />`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup prop mismatch and missing errorId in NewsletterForm email field
issuesFound.explanation: FormGroup is passed the prop `error` but its interface expects `errorMessage`, so the error text is never passed to the ErrorMessage sub-component. Additionally, `errorId` is missing, so the aria-describedby attribute on the TextInput points to a non-existent ID, breaking the programmatic association between the input and its error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1019-1022: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} required>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup prop mismatch and missing errorId in ContactForm name field
issuesFound.explanation: FormGroup receives `error` but expects `errorMessage`, preventing the error from rendering. The `errorId` prop is also absent, causing the TextInput's aria-describedby to reference a missing element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1079-1082: `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup prop mismatch and missing errorId in ContactForm email field

issuesFound.fix: Line 1093-1096: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup prop mismatch and missing errorId in ContactForm subject field
issuesFound.explanation: FormGroup is passed `error` instead of `errorMessage`, suppressing the error message. The `errorId` prop is missing, so aria-describedby on the TextInput points to nothing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1107-1110: `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup prop mismatch and missing errorId in ContactForm message field

issuesFound.fix: Line 1121-1124: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`}>`

issuesFound.explanation: The submit button has aria-describedby="newsletter-status", but the ErrorMessage component is invoked with children instead of the required message prop, causing it to render nothing. Consequently, no element with id "newsletter-status" exists in the DOM, so screen readers cannot access the server error message.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div has onClick without keyboard accessibility support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the modal, but it lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler. Keyboard users cannot activate this control, and screen reader users may not perceive it as interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>` to `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose} role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} aria-label="Close modal">`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal overlay div instead of the dialog element itself. This causes assistive technologies to treat the overlay as the modal container rather than the actual dialog, potentially allowing users to navigate outside the dialog content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1: Remove aria-modal="true" from `<div className="modal-overlay" ...>` 
  Fix: Line 2: Add aria-modal="true" to `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" ...>` resulting in `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input error message not associated via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby referencing 'newsletter-email-error' when validation fails, but the FormGroup component is passed the error message via the prop name 'error' instead of the expected 'errorMessage', and the errorId prop is omitted entirely. This prevents the ErrorMessage component from rendering with the correct ID, breaking the association between the input and its error message for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
<FormGroup
    label="Email address"
    htmlFor="newsletter-email"
    errorId="newsletter-email-error"
    errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >
    <TextInput
      id="newsletter-email"
      type="email"
      name="email"
      placeholder="you@company.com"
      autoComplete="email"
      value={email.state.value}
      onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
      onBlur={email.onBlur}
      required
      aria-required="true"
      invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
      aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}
    />
  </FormGroup>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input error message not associated via aria-describedby

Consequently, the error message element is never created with the referenced ID, leaving screen readers unable to announce the error for this field.

      required

      invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
      aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}
    />
  </FormGroup>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea error message not associated via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea references 'contact-message-error' in its aria-describedby array, but FormGroup is not provided with the errorId prop and receives the error via the incorrectly named 'error' prop instead of 'errorMessage'. This prevents the error message from being rendered with the required ID, leaving screen readers unable to announce the validation error for the message field.
issuesFound.fix:

    label="Message"
    htmlFor="contact-message"
    required
    hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`}
    errorId="contact-message-error"
    errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}
  >

      id="contact-message"
      name="message"
      rows={6}

      aria-required="true"
      value={message.state.value}
      onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)}

      invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error}
      aria-describedby={[
        'contact-message-hint',
        counterId,
        message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : '',
      ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
    />

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm ErrorMessage usage with children instead of message prop
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a message prop but receives children. The component returns null when message is undefined, so the error text is not rendered to the DOM and screen readers cannot announce the server error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm ErrorMessage usage with children instead of message prop
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a message prop but receives children. The component returns null when message is undefined, so the error text is not rendered and screen users cannot perceive the submission failure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2252: <ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." />

issuesFound.title: Modal component aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. Screen readers may not recognize the dialog as modal, allowing users to navigate outside the dialog while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2316: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> Line 2318: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby pointing to 'newsletter-email-error', but FormGroup only renders the ErrorMessage element when the errorId prop is provided, which is missing. This creates a broken ARIA reference that points to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change FormGroup props from error={...} to errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}

issuesFound.fix: Line: Add errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup props.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The label element in FormGroup receives the htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element, so the label is not programmatically associated with the input. Screen readers will not announce the label when users focus the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 549: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar decorative clone list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integration logos used for visual marquee scrolling is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element with role="dialog", causing assistive technologies to potentially include background content in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

fpTitles: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
Modal overlay div onClick without keyboard accessibility
